title: Repair contract CI path filters
goal: Ensure real contract paths trigger validation and remove or explain dead path filters

acceptance.assertion: The slice is implemented or explicitly closed against current main/PR state with no hidden broadening.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: RepoBrief boundaries are preserved: no hidden refresh, no Git mutation in RepoBrief core, no review/runtime correctness claim.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: Relevant tests/checks are run, or a precise blocker/gap is recorded.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: The output states what the evidence does not establish.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

metadata.outcome: Lenskit PR #905 repaired contract validation path filtering by removing workflow-level path filters and relying on the contract guard for the real contract roots.
validation: GitHub CI after PR #905 passed on Lenskit main as observed before closeout.

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t012-scope-bound: Contract CI path filter reconciliation was implemented in Lenskit PR #905 without broadening RepoBrief authority.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t012-boundary-preserved: The change is CI-only and does not create refresh, Git mutation, review, runtime or RepoBrief write authority.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t012-tests-or-gap: Workflow guard coverage and CI validation were recorded in Lenskit task closeout.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t012-non-claims: The CI path repair does not prove contract correctness, fixture completeness, runtime correctness, test sufficiency or release readiness.
